zh.Framersheim, Rheinland-Pfalz, Germany: 德國海藍-普法爾茲藍德可萊斯 阿爾澤-喬姆斯福拉莫斯海姆
zh.Framersheim, Alzey-Worms, Rheinland-Pfal: 德國海藍-普法爾茲藍德可萊斯 阿爾澤-喬姆斯福拉莫斯海姆

zh.Framersheim, Framersheim, Alzey-Worms, R: 德國海藍-普法爾茲藍德可萊斯 阿爾澤-喬姆斯福拉莫斯海姆
zh.lifeSummary: 當Johann Jacob Wendel於大約1650年出生在德國海藍-普法爾茲時，他的父親Philipp Wendel32歲，母親Anna27歲。他於大約1678年與Mrs. Johann Jacob Wendel在德國結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1693年9月在德國海藍-普法爾茲藍德可萊斯 阿爾澤-喬姆斯福拉莫斯海姆享年44歲，埋葬在德國。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Johann Jacob Wendel于大约1650年出生在德国海藍-普法爾茲时，他的父亲Philipp Wendel32岁，母亲Anna27岁。他于大约1678年与Mrs. Johann Jacob Wendel在德国结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1693年9月在德国海藍-普法爾茲藍德可萊斯 阿爾澤-喬姆斯福拉莫斯海姆享年44岁，埋葬在德国。

nameMeanings.name: Wendel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Helmut, Otto, Aloys, Bodo, Egon, Elke, Erwin, Frieda, Gerhard, Jurgen, Katharina.
German, Swedish, Danish, and Dutch: from the German personal name Wendel, a pet form of a short form, Wando, of a compound ancient Germanic name beginning with wand ‘to go, to migrate’.
German: metonymic occupational name for a turner, from Middle High German wendel ‘to turn’ or Middle Low German wendelen ‘to turn, twist’.

variants.name: Wendelin
meanings: South German: from the personal name Wendelin, a pet form of Wendel . Wendelin is the name of a Christian saint venerated in the Saarland, Germany.
Swedish: habitational name from a place called Vendel + the adjectival suffix -in, a derivative of Latin -in(i)us ‘relating to’. In some cases it may also be of German origin (see 1 above).

variants.name: Wendl
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans.
South German (Bavaria and Austria): variant of   Wendel  .

variants.name: Wendler
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Arno, Kurt, Siegbert, Claus, Erwin, Ewald, Gerd, Hans, Helmut, Helmuth, Otto.
German:
from Middle High German wandeler ‘traveler on foot’ (Middle Low German wendeler), hence a nickname for a pilgrim or an occupational name for a peddler.

variants.name: Wandell
meanings: Variant of   Wendel  .

variants.name: Wendell
meanings: German, Swedish, and Danish: variant of Wendel and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this (which can be also of Dutch origin). The surname Wendell is (very) rare in Germany, Sweden, and Denmark.
History: A prominent New Netherland (now NY) merchant family of this name descended from a colonist from Emden, Germany, which was part of the Netherlands in the 17th century.

variants.name: Wendle
meanings: South German: variant of   Wendel   and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this.

variants.name: Wandel
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Gerhard, Horst, Klaus.
German and Dutch: variant of   Wendel  .
Flemish (De Wandel): nickname for a pilgrim or an occupational name for a peddler, or a nickname for an unreliable person.

variants.name: Windels
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Gerhard, Lothar.
German: patronymic from (a variant of) the personal name   Wendel  .
North German: metronymic from a short form of the female personal names Windelheid or Windelmod.

variants.name: Wendling
meanings: German:
habitational name from any of numerous places called Wendling, notably in Bavaria, or a place called Wendlingen, for example near Stuttgart.
(Westphalia): patronymic from the personal name Wendel (see Wendel 1).

nameMeanings.name: Johann
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Vernacular form of   Johannes .

variants.name: John
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

nameMeanings.name: Jacob
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, from Hebrew Yaakov. This was borne by perhaps the most important of all the patriarchs in the Book of Genesis. Jacob was the father of twelve sons, who gave their names to the twelve tribes of Israel. He was the son of Isaac and Rebecca and twin brother of Esau. According to the story in Genesis, he was the cunning younger twin, who persuaded his brother Esau to part with his right to his inheritance in exchange for a bowl of soup (‘a mess of pottage’). Later, he tricked his blind and dying father into blessing him in place of Esau. The derivation of the name has been much discussed. It is traditionally explained as being derived from Hebrew akev ‘heel’ and to have meant ‘heel grabber’, because when Jacob was born ‘his hand took hold of Esau's heel’ (Genesis 25:26). This is interpreted later in the Bible as ‘supplanter’ Esau himself remarks, ‘Is he not rightly named Jacob? for he has supplanted me these two times’ (Genesis 27:36). Jacob is especially common as a Jewish given name, although it also became very popular among the Puritans from the 16th century onwards, and has again been widely used since the 1990s. Compare James .

en.lifeSummary: When Johann Jacob Wendel was born about 1650, in Rhineland-Palatinate, Germany, his father, Philipp Wendel, was 32 and his mother, Anna, was 27. He married Mrs. Johann Jacob Wendel about 1678, in Germany. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. He died in September 1693, in Framersheim, Alzey-Worms, Rhineland-Palatinate, Germany, at the age of 44, and was buried in Germany.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Johann Jacob Wendel est né environ 1650, à Rheinland-Pfalz, Allemagne, son père, Philipp Wendel, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Anna, avait 27 ans. Il a épousé Mrs. Johann Jacob Wendel environ 1678, à Allemagne. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé en septembre 1693, à Framersheim, Landkreis Alzey-Worms, Rheinland-Pfalz, Allemagne, à l’âge de 44 ans, et a été enterré à Allemagne.

de.lifeSummary: Als Johann Jacob Wendel um 1650 in Rheinland-Pfalz, Deutschland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Philipp Wendel 32 und seine Mutter Anna 27. Er hat um 1678 in Deutschland Mrs. Johann Jacob Wendel geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Er starb im September 1693 im Alter von 44 Jahren in Framersheim, Landkreis Alzey-Worms, Rheinland-Pfalz, Deutschland und wurde in Deutschland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Johann Jacob Wendel è nato il circa 1650 nel Rheinland-Pfalz, Germania, il suo padre, Philipp Wendel, aveva 32 anni e il suo madre, Anna, aveva 27 anni. Lui sposato Mrs. Johann Jacob Wendel circa 1678, nel Germania. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia. Morì nel settembre 1693, nel Framersheim, Landkreis Alzey-Worms, Rheinland-Pfalz, Germania, all'età di 44 anni, e fu sepolto a Germania.

ja.lifeSummary: Johann Jacob Wendelが約1650年にドイツルヘインランド-プファルズで生まれた時，父Philipp Wendelは32歳，母Annaは27歳であった。約1678年にドイツでMrs. Johann Jacob Wendelと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1693年9月にドイツルヘインランド-プファルズランドクレイス アルズエイ-ウオルムズフラムルシェイムで44歳で死去し，ドイツにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Johann Jacob Wendel(이)가 대략 1650년 독일 리인랜드-p팔즈에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Philipp Wendel(은)는 32세였고, 그의 어머니 Anna(은)는 27세였다. 그는 대략 1678년 독일에서 Mrs. Johann Jacob Wendel(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1693년 9월 독일 리인랜드-p팔즈 랜드크레이스 알즈이-워스 프레이머셰임에서 44세의 나이로 사망했으며 독일에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Johann Jacob Wendel nasceu aproximadamente 1650, em Rheinland-Pfalz, Alemanha, seu pai, Philipp Wendel, tinha 32 anos e sua mãe, Anna, tinha 27 anos. Ele casou-se com Mrs. Johann Jacob Wendel aproximadamente 1678, em Alemanha. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em setembro de 1693, em Framersheim, Landkreis Alzey-Worms, Rheinland-Pfalz, Alemanha, com 44 anos, e foi sepultado em Alemanha.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Johann Jacob Wendel родился около 1650 г. в местности раинланд-пфалз, Германия, его отцу по имени Philipp Wendel было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Anna, 27. Он женился на женщине по имени Mrs. Johann Jacob Wendel около 1678 г. в местности Германия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Он умер сентябрь 1693 г. в местности фрамиршим, ландкрис алзи-вормс, раинланд-пфалз, Германия в возрасте 44 лет и был похоронен в местности Германия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Johann Jacob Wendel nació aproximadamente 1650, en Rheinland-Pfalz, Alemania, su papá, Philipp Wendel, tenía 32 años y su mamá, Anna, tenía 27 años. Se casó con Mrs. Johann Jacob Wendel aproximadamente 1678, en Alemania. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 1 hija. Murió en septiembre de 1693, en Framersheim, Landkreis Alzey-Worms, Rheinland-Pfalz, Alemania, a la edad de 44 años, y fue sepultado en Alemania.
